title: Decoupling Structure and Objects for Multi-View 3D Scene Reconstruction
teaser: Develop a multi-view scene reconstruction pipeline combining feed-forward reconstruction from DA3 for structural elements (floor, walls, ceiling) with per-object reconstruction via SAM3D/MV-SAM3D.
abstract: Multi-view 3D scene reconstruction is challenging because occluded regions often cause artifacts and broken geometry. We decouple these into two specialized sub-tasks: structural reconstruction and per-object reconstruction. For structure, we remove objects from the input images using an image diffusion model (e.g. FLUX.2) and run Depth Anything 3 on the cleaned images to recover walls, floors, and ceilings. For objects, we reconstruct each instance with SAM-3D (alternatively: MV-SAM-3D, RecGen) together with their initial pose. We then train a seperate module to combine the structural reconstruction with the per-object reconstructions.
milestones: Remove objects from the input images using FLUX.2 and set up a multi-view Depth Anything 3 pipeline for initial structural scene geometry.
Use SAM-3D (alternatively: MV-SAM-3D, RecGen) to reconstruct each object instance separately.
Train a separate combination module that refines the initial SAM-3D object poses to match the structural geometry.
Alternative: try using an agent to iteratively refine the placement of the objects within the structural reconstruction.

prerequisites.label: Advanced Deep Learning for Computer Vision: Visual Computing (ADL4CV)

references.label: Depth Anything 3: Recovering the Visual Space from Any Views
